summary:
The child likely suffers from Tubercular Meningitis given his presentation including meningeal irritation signs, altered mental status over time, single seizure event followed by coma, elevated white blood count without significant neutrophilia, low cerebrospinal fluid sugar level consistent with chronic inflammation typical seen in TB infection as opposed to acute bacterial infections like Pyogenic Meningoencephalitides which usually present acutely with high WBCs predominantly Neutrophils alongwith raised proteins & decreased sugars transiently.
The differential diagnoses include Cryptococcus neoformans or Toxoplasmosis especially if immunocompromised; however, based upon current information provided it aligns most closely with suspected case of Tuberculosis related CNS pathology requiring urgent anti-tubercular therapy alongside supportive care measures such hydration management etc., pending further confirmatory investigations like India The differential diagnoses include Cryptococcus neoformans or Toxoplasmosis especially if immunocompromised; however, based upon current information provided it aligns most closely with suspected case
